methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $5M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Nox Metals is a Seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by Zane Hengsperger that has raised $5.1M to date. The broader Robotics & Manufacturing sector comprises 9 comparable companies (1 public, 8 private), including peers such as 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics. However, the peer set lacks sufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables to support a reliable Peer-Median valuation approach.

Consequently, the valuation relies on Stage-Progression methodology, which applies a 2.5x multiplier to the $5.1M total raised as a proxy for current company value. This step-up factor reflects typical value creation expectations from Seed to subsequent financing stages within the sector. The method yields an implied valuation of $13M.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $11M to $15M, centered on $13M. The median estimate reflects the sole applicable quantitative method; the range incorporates reasonable variance around stage-progression assumptions. Key uncertainties include the absence of narrow-set peer data for calibration and the reliance on a single methodological pillar. Resolution of valuation would benefit from either identifying same-stage robotics comparables or obtaining more granular financial metrics (revenue, growth rate, unit economics) from the target.
